fields.narrative_intro: *A manic person and a scowling person discuss Claude Code's Extended Thinking logs at a 3am Walmart.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
MANIC PERSON: "Compliance needs Claude's reasoning. I exported the thinking logs!"
fields.panel2:
[build]
SCOWLING PERSON: "That's a summary. The raw thinking got discarded."
MANIC PERSON: "A summary's basically the same thing, right?"
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
SCOWLING PERSON: "It's lossy. Like a JPEG. And there's a 600-character encrypted blob."
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: Manic person holds the printout up to a flickering Walmart fluorescent light, squinting at the encrypted gibberish. Scowling person stares.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
MANIC PERSON: "So I'll just decrypt it!"
SCOWLING PERSON: "Only Anthropic can. You need an enterprise agreement."
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: Manic person at self-checkout scanning the printout. Screen reads: 'CURATED PERFORMANCE — $0.00'. Scowling person walks away.]

narrative_summary: An eager dev tries to use Claude Code's Extended Thinking logs as an audit trail, only to escalate through every wall — it's a lossy summary, an undecryptable signature, and enterprise-locked — landing on the reveal that they audited a performance, not actual reasoning.
rationale: Candidate 4 uses the escalation voice to actually build through each wall (lossy summary, encrypted blob, enterprise lock) so the template earns its shape, and the '$0.00 curated performance' self-checkout button lands as a real reveal rather than restating the premise. Candidate 5's receipt-printer sight gag is fresher, but its punchline mostly recaps; the escalation structure gives 4 the edge.
story_title: The text in Claude Code's "Extended Thinking" output is not authentic
hat_pair: shower cap covered in sticky notes
powdered barrister's wig with a tiny gavel taped to the top
